title: 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules
text:
Welcome
The Concept and Application of Bioactive Molecules (CAMBio) Laboratory, in partnership with the National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB), is pleased to welcome you to the 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules: From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications.
This inaugural seminar will bring together researchers, academics, students, and industrial partners to exchange recent advances in bioactive molecules and their innovative applications in biotechnology.
The seminar will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend either on-site at ENSB or online.

title: Scientific Program
text:
The 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules will take place over two days, bringing together keynote lectures, oral presentations, poster sessions, and scientific discussions.

Dates: November 29–30, 2026
Venue: National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB), Constantine, Algeria

title: Registration
text:
We are pleased to invite researchers, academics, students, and professionals to participate in the 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules: From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications.
The seminar will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend either in person at the National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB), Constantine, or online.






Category
Registration Fee




PhD Students
3,000 DA


Professors & Researchers
6,000 DA


Industrial Participants
9,000 DA







The registration fee covers:

Participation in all scientific sessions
Access to keynote lectures
Conference materials
Certificate of participation
Coffee breaks
Book of Abstracts (digital version)


Note: Lunch and accommodation are not included unless otherwise specified.







Complete the online registration form.

Registration Form: (Available Soon)





Pay the registration fee according to your participant category.
Payment instructions will be communicated after registration.




After payment confirmation, you will receive

Registration confirmation
Payment receipt
Practical information
Access information for online participants

title: Venue
text:
The 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules (NSBM 2026) will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend in person at the National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB), Constantine, Algeria, or online from anywhere in the world.



Seminar Venue






TipNational Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB)



École Nationale Supérieure de Biotechnologie (ENSB) – Taoufik Khaznadar
📍 Ville Universitaire Ali Mendjeli
BP E66 RP 25.100
Constantine, Algeria
☎ Telephone: +213 (0)31 786 011
📠 Fax: +213 (0)31 786 010
🌐 Website: https://www.ensbiotech.edu.dz





About ENSB
The National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB) is one of Algeria’s leading institutions dedicated to biotechnology education, scientific research, and innovation.
Located within the Ali Mendjeli University Campus, ENSB offers modern teaching facilities, advanced laboratories, and an inspiring academic environment. Its location in Constantine, one of Algeria’s major scientific and cultural centers, makes it an ideal venue for bringing together researchers, academics, students, and industry professionals.



Participation








Important🏛️ On-site Participation



Participants attending in person will be able to:

Attend keynote lectures
Present oral and poster communications
Participate in scientific discussions
Meet researchers and industry professionals
Build new collaborations and networks










Note💻 Online Participation



Remote participants will receive:

Secure access to the online platform
Live streaming of scientific sessions
Electronic conference materials
Certificate of participation








Getting to Constantine
Constantine is easily accessible from all regions of Algeria.

✈️ Mohamed Boudiaf International Airport
🚆 National railway services
🚌 Intercity bus network
🚖 Taxi services

ENSB is located in the Ali Mendjeli University Campus, approximately 20 minutes from Constantine city centre and the international airport.



Accommodation
Participants are encouraged to reserve accommodation as early as possible.
Hotels are available near the university campus as well as in the city centre.
A list of recommended hotels and negotiated conference rates will be published soon.



Interactive Map






Note



Locate the seminar venue on Google Maps:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/





Discover Constantine

Known as the City of Bridges, Constantine is one of the oldest and most fascinating cities in North Africa.
During your stay, you may wish to visit:

🌉 Sidi M’Cid Suspension Bridge
🏛️ Palace of Ahmed Bey
🕌 Emir Abdelkader Mosque
🏺 Cirta National Museum
🌄 Rhumel Gorges
🍽️ Traditional restaurants and cafés




Contact
For any questions regarding travel, accommodation, or the venue, please contact:
Seminar Secretariat
📧 nsbm@ensbiotech.edu.dz
☎ +213 5 50 84 74 87







ImportantWe Look Forward to Welcoming You!



Whether you join us in Constantine or online, we look forward to welcoming you to the 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules (NSBM 2026) and wish you a productive and enjoyable scientific experience.

title: Venue
text:
The National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB) is one of Algeria’s leading institutions dedicated to higher education, scientific research, and innovation in biotechnology.
Located in the university city of Ali Mendjeli, Constantine, ENSB provides an excellent academic environment equipped with modern teaching and research facilities. Its strategic location in Constantine, one of Algeria’s major academic centers, makes it an ideal venue for hosting scientific events and fostering collaboration between researchers, students, and industry partners.

title: Venue
text:
Participants may choose one of two participation modes.


Join us at ENSB in Constantine to

Attend keynote lectures
Participate in scientific sessions
Present oral or poster communications
Meet researchers and industry professionals
Network with participants





Participants unable to travel can join remotely through the seminar’s online platform.
Online participants will receive

Access links before the seminar

Live streaming of scientific sessions
